revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=田中・舟森山古墳| 所在地=福島県喜多方市塩川町中屋沢| 形状=前方後方（円）墳| 規模=全長70m、高さ7m| 築造年代=4世紀| 出土品=埴輪、土師器| }}
'''田中・舟森山古墳'''（たなか・ふなもりやまこふん）は、[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]の[[雄国山麓古墳群]]内にある[[前方後方墳]]である。
==概要== 現在の墳丘は楕円形をしているが、住宅街の中にある古墳のため、墳形が著しく変形しているほか、墳頂も大幅に削平されている。消失してしまったが前方部があった可能性が高く、前方後方墳もしくは前方後円墳であったとみられる。もし前者なら東北地方最大の前方後方墳ということになる。塩川町教育委員会による試掘調査では墳頂から粘土槨が検出された。
==副葬品== 埴輪と[[土師器]]が出土している。
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:たなか・ふなもりやまこふん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:喜多方市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Japanese Kofun |Name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |Location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:田中・舟森山古墳]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:田中・舟森山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:田中・舟森山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:anaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:田中・舟森山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:anaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:田中・舟森山古墳]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:anaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanaka-Funamori-Yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
