{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |Artifacts=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |Scale=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |Construction Period=4th century |burial goods=Haniwa, Haji Ware |Map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==

revision.comment: attempt at mass correcting bad parameters on indofox ancient site
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |Shape=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |map_size=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |built=4th century |burial goods=Haniwa, Haji Ware |map2 = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. '''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-Shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |map_size=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |built=4th century |burial goods=Haniwa, Haji Ware |map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Single source|date=October 21, 2013}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} |name=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun |image={{image requested|cat=Kitakata City}} |location={{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市|12=draft}} Shinkawa Town, Nakayasawa |coordinates = {{Coord|37|36|05.3|N|139|54|48.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |map_size=Total length: 70 m, height: 7 m |built=4th century |burial goods=Haniwa, Haji Ware |map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Okunokoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kitakata City|ja|喜多方市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}, Japan.
== Overview ==
The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.
A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
== References ==
* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|qq=}} in [[:en:Kitakata, Fukushima|Kitakata City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan.

== Overview ==

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.

A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

== References ==

* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|qq=}} in [[:en:Kitakata, Fukushima|Kitakata City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan.

== Overview ==

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.

A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

== References ==

* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|qq=}} in [[:en:Kitakata, Fukushima|Kitakata City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan.

== Overview ==

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.

A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

== References ==

* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|qq=}} in [[:en:Kitakata, Fukushima|Kitakata City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan.

== Overview ==

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.

A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

== References ==

* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

'''Tanaka Funamoriyama Kofun''' (田中・舟森山古墳) is a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] located within the {{ill|Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|ja|雄国山麓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Foothills Kofun Group|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitakata, Fukushima|en|Kitakata, Fukushima|WD=Q496506|lt=Kitakata City}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan.

== Overview ==

The current mound has an elliptical shape but is significantly deformed due to its location within a residential area. The original form of the mound has been substantially altered, with the apex heavily leveled. Although the frontal section is now lost, it is believed that the mound originally had a frontal section, suggesting it was either a [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) or a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped Mound). If the former, it would represent the largest Zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Tōhoku region.

A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.

== References ==

* Tsuji, Hidetoshi. ''Tōhoku Kofun Research Origins: The Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun''. Series: "Learning from Sites" 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

A trial excavation conducted by the Shinkawa Town Board of Education revealed the presence of a clay burial chamber (''nenkaku'') at the apex of the mound. Based on haniwa and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} artifacts excavated from the site, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the 4th century.
